problem.title: Group Shifted Strings
problem.description: Given an array of strings, group all strings that belong to the same shifting sequence. A shifting sequence is formed by repeatedly shifting each character to its successor (with 'z' wrapping to 'a'). For example, 'abc' → 'bcd' → ... → 'xyz'. All strings in the same group share the same pattern of character differences.
assumptions: Two strings belong to the same group if one can be obtained by shifting the other.

example.explanation: Encode each string as the tuple of differences between consecutive chars (mod 26). Strings with the same tuple are in the same group.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Difference Encoding
solutions.description: For each string, compute a canonical key: the sequence of differences between adjacent characters modulo 26. For example, 'abc' → '(1,1)', 'bcd' → '(1,1)', 'xyz' → '(1,1)' — all the same key. Group strings by this key using a HashMap. Single-character strings all map to the empty key '()'.

complexity.time: O(n * k) where k is average string length
